name: frontend-developer
description: Build React components, implement responsive layouts, and handle
capabilities: Build React 19 components with Server Components and Actions patterns
Implement Next.js 15 App Router with parallel and intercepting routes
Optimize Core Web Vitals including LCP, FID, and CLS metrics
Create accessible UI components following WCAG 2.1 AA standards
Design state management solutions with Zustand, Jotai, or React Query
Configure Tailwind CSS with design tokens and theme systems
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only instructional text in SKILL.md. Static analysis scanned 0 files with 0 lines of executable code and detected no security patterns. The skill provides frontend development guidance for React and Next.js without any code execution, network access, or file system operations. No malicious patterns or prompt injection attempts detected.
valueStatement: Frontend development requires deep expertise in React, performance optimization, and accessibility standards. This skill provides production-ready guidance for building scalable web applications with modern tools and best practices.
userTitle: Build modern React and Next.js applications
limitations: Cannot execute or test code in a live browser environment
Cannot directly modify files without Claude Code tool access
Cannot preview visual designs or run performance profiling tools
Relies on user to validate browser compatibility and device testing

useCases.title: React Component Development
useCases.description: Build production-ready React components with TypeScript, proper hooks usage, and optimized rendering patterns for scalable applications.

useCases.title: Next.js Application Architecture
useCases.description: Design full-stack Next.js applications with Server Components, Server Actions, and optimal data fetching strategies.

useCases.title: Performance Optimization
useCases.description: Analyze and improve frontend performance through code splitting, lazy loading, and Core Web Vitals optimization techniques.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Component Creation
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a reusable React button component with TypeScript props, supporting variants (primary, secondary), sizes, and disabled state. Include Tailwind CSS classes and proper accessibility attributes.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building a design system component library

promptTemplates.title: Server Component with Data Fetching
promptTemplates.prompt: Build a Next.js 15 Server Component that fetches product data from an API, implements Suspense boundaries for streaming, and handles loading and error states gracefully.

promptTemplates.title: Form with Server Actions
promptTemplates.prompt: Implement a contact form using Next.js Server Actions with Zod validation, optimistic updates using useOptimistic, and proper error handling with user feedback.
promptTemplates.scenario: Building interactive forms with server-side validation

promptTemplates.title: Performance Audit and Optimization
promptTemplates.prompt: Analyze this React component for performance issues. Identify unnecessary re-renders, suggest memoization strategies, and provide optimized code with useMemo, useCallback, and React.memo where appropriate.
promptTemplates.scenario: Optimizing existing components for better rendering performance

bestPractices: Always include TypeScript types for props, state, and API responses to catch errors early
Implement proper loading states and error boundaries for all async operations
Use semantic HTML elements and ARIA attributes to ensure accessibility compliance
antiPatterns: Avoid using useEffect for data fetching when React Query or Server Components are available
Do not store server state in React state when dedicated data fetching libraries exist
Never skip accessibility testing or assume default HTML behavior is sufficient

faq.question: What React version does this skill support?
faq.answer: This skill specializes in React 19+ features including Server Components, Actions, useActionState, useOptimistic, and concurrent rendering patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill help with Next.js 15 migration?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill provides guidance on migrating from Pages Router to App Router, implementing Server Components, and adopting Next.js 15 best practices.

faq.question: Does this skill support state management libraries?
faq.answer: Yes, expertise includes Zustand, Jotai, Valtio, Redux Toolkit, React Query, SWR, and Context API optimization patterns.

faq.question: Can this skill help with accessibility compliance?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill implements WCAG 2.1/2.2 AA standards, ARIA patterns, keyboard navigation, focus management, and screen reader optimization.

faq.question: What testing approaches does this skill recommend?
faq.answer: This skill recommends React Testing Library for unit tests, Playwright or Cypress for E2E testing, and Storybook for component documentation and visual regression.

faq.question: Does this skill support CSS frameworks?
faq.answer: Yes, expertise includes Tailwind CSS, CSS Modules, CSS-in-JS solutions like emotion and styled-components, and design token integration.
